NetScope · AI Network Analyzer · v3

One tool.
Capture · Decode · Detect · Explain.

AI-powered PCAP analysis dashboard with live switch capture, multi-layer anomaly detection, and an in-browser AI Protocol Analyst — running entirely from two Python files, zero CDN.

📡 Live EXOS Capture 🔬 L1–L7 Parsing 🚨 Hybrid Anomaly Detection 🤖 RAG + LLM Pipeline 🔌 MCP Live Switch Query ⚡ SNMP Trap Analysis
40+
Protocols Decoded
L2 → L7, binary parser
30+
Anomaly Rules
Rule + ML + LLM
7
Dashboard Views
Zero CDN dependencies
4
AI Pipeline Steps
Sequential LLM reasoning
3
AI Backends
Ollama · Claude · OpenAI
2
Python Files
stdlib-only core
Core Workflow

How NetScope works — step by step

📥
01
Input Layer Data Ingestion

Four entry points — PCAP file upload (drag & drop or CLI), live switch capture via SSH/Telnet, SNMP trap CSV/JSON from trap managers, or trap packets embedded in a PCAP. All paths funnel into the same unified analysis pipeline.

--pcap FILE --switch IP POST /upload Trap CSV upload PCAP slot comparison
PCAP / PCAPNG
Upload via UI drag-and-drop, CLI --pcap flag, or POST /upload endpoint. Both endian formats supported.
Live Switch Capture
SSH or Telnet to EXOS switch → SFTP download of pcap file. 4-phase background capture with live progress polling.
SNMP Trap CSV/JSON
Import from trap managers. Auto-detects format. Triggers dedicated snmp_trap_analyzer.py pipeline.
Multi-Slot
Two PCAP slots (original/uploaded) — switch between them without re-parsing via /api/switch-pcap.
🔬
02
Parse Engine Binary Packet Parser read_pcap() → _parse_one() × N

Pure-Python binary PCAP reader — no Scapy, no Wireshark dependency at runtime. Decodes every byte from Ethernet frame to application payload, building a structured packet dict per frame. Optional tshark enrichment pass adds DHCP message type names, SNMP OID strings, tunnel decodes, and any protocol beyond the binary parser's coverage.

Ethernet · 802.1Q VLAN ARP · RARP IPv4 · IPv6 TCP · UDP · ICMP · IGMP SNMP ASN.1/BER DNS · DHCP · NTP · SIP · BGP tshark enrichment (optional)
EXOS Header Strip
_exos_offset() auto-detects and strips EXOS proprietary capture headers (0–52 byte offsets) per packet.
App Protocol Classification
_classify_app_protocol() maps port + transport → app protocol. Payload heuristics confirm (e.g. TLS record bytes, HTTP verbs).
SNMP ASN.1 Decode
_detect_snmp() parses BER-encoded SNMP payloads without tshark — extracts version, community, PDU type, trap OID.
tshark Enrichment
_run_tshark() → _merge_tshark() adds DHCP message names, DNS record details, VXLAN/GRE decodes, SNMP varbind OID strings.
⚡
03
Parallel Path SNMP Trap Analysis snmp_trap_analyzer.py

Runs parallel to the main pipeline. Ingests trap packets from PCAP or a standalone CSV/JSON export from your trap manager. Correlates trap streams to detect link flaps, auth storms, config-change storms, and stacking events. Generates a standalone 6-section Traps tab HTML with a 0–100 risk score.

Link flap detection (30s window) Auth-failure storm (≥3 in 60s) Config-change storm (≥5 in 60s) Risk Score 0–100 SNMPv3 coverage tracking Weak community warning
TrapRecord dataclass
frame_id, timestamp, src_ip, version, community, pdu_type, enterprise OID, generic_type, specific_code, varbinds, vendor, severity.
Risk Score Weights
Security events ×15 (max 40), link flaps ×10 (max 20), auth storms ×15 (max 30), config storms ×8 (max 16), weak community +10, no SNMPv3 +8.
HTML Output
6 sections: stat cards, vendor/severity charts (Canvas2D), trap events table, agents table, security panel, AI analysis button.
Graceful Degradation
Imported lazily (try/except). If snmp_trap_analyzer.py is absent, Traps tab shows a placeholder — main pipeline unaffected.
📊
04
Feature Extraction L2–L7 Statistical Aggregation _extract_features()

Transforms the flat packet list into a rich feature dictionary consumed by the Rule Engine, ML Scorer, and AI Pipeline. Builds MAC frequency tables, TCP flow maps, DNS query baselines, DHCP state machines, TTL histograms, and per-protocol session graphs across all OSI layers.

L2: MAC rate, broadcast %, ARP table L3: TTL histogram, fragmentation, DSCP L4: SYN/ACK ratio, RST rate, port fan-out L5-6: TLS cipher, session count L7: DNS rate, DHCP state machine, SNMP OIDs
TCP Flow Map
SYN/FIN/RST/retransmit counts per (src_ip, src_port, dst_ip, dst_port) 4-tuple. Half-open detection.
DNS Entropy
Per-query entropy score (unique chars / length). High-entropy long domains flagged for DNS tunneling analysis.
DHCP State Machine
Tracks DISCOVER/OFFER/REQUEST/ACK/NAK per client. Starvation detection: 10 requests in 5s window.
IP→MAC Map
Maps each IP to all seen MACs. Multiple MACs per IP = ARP spoofing / MITM indicator.
🚨
05
Anomaly Detection 3-Layer Hybrid Detection Engine

Three independent engines run in sequence — deterministic rules catch known patterns instantly, statistical ML flags statistical deviations without training data, and cross-layer correlation elevates severity when multiple independent signals converge (e.g. SYN flood + RST storm = confirmed DoS). Every finding is a structured dict with severity, OSI layer, category, title, detail, and evidence.

Rule Engine: 30+ L2–L7 rules ML: z-score deviation, graph fan-out Cross-layer: 5 composite patterns Severity: critical/high/medium/low/info
Rule Engine Samples
ARP storm, SYN flood, RST storm, port scan (15+ dst ports), NULL/XMAS scan, DHCP starvation, rogue DHCP, DNS tunneling (high entropy), MAC flood, broadcast storm >30%.
ML Scoring
No external sklearn needed. Z-score-equivalent for packet rate, DNS query rate, flow size. Per-capture baselines. Confidence score 0–100 per finding.
Cross-Layer Patterns
MAC Spoof (L2+L3), Confirmed DoS (SYN+RST), Network Takeover (DHCP starvation+rogue), Traffic Diversion (routing+traffic shift), Auth→Scan chain.
Finding Structure
{title, detail, severity, layer, category, evidence, ai_confirmed, ai_reason, ai_mitigation}
📚
06
RAG · Knowledge Base RFC + EXOS Knowledge Retrieval _RAGRetriever

Before any AI call reaches the LLM, the RAG engine retrieves verified RFC + EXOS CLI knowledge grounded to the exact protocols detected in the PCAP. Eliminates hallucinated CLI commands. Built-in TF-IDF index (zero deps) upgrades to sentence-transformers dense embeddings if available. Unknown protocols get an explicit disclaimer instead of confabulated answers.

TF-IDF baseline (stdlib only) sentence-transformers upgrade Protocol direct lookup (priority 1) Semantic search (priority 2) 8 chunks max / 6000 char limit Unknown protocol disclaimer Runbook ingestion (knowledge_base/)
Built-in KB Protocols
EAPoL, RADIUS, ARP, STP, LLDP, TCP, ICMP, DHCP, DNS, SNMP, OSPF, BGP, VLAN, LACP/MLAG, IGMP, IPv6, NTP, VRRP — with EXOS CLI for each.
Chunk Structure
{id, title, text, tags, source, rfc, layer} — direct protocol match scores 1.0, semantic results scored 0–1 by cosine similarity.
Inject Point
Context prepended before user prompt as "## RFC Reference Material" block. AI instructed to prefer this over training weights.
Extend KB
knowledge_base/rfc_index.json (JSON), knowledge_base/runbooks/*.txt/.md files auto-ingested. add_runbook_chunk() API for runtime additions.
🤖
07
AI Pipeline 4-Step Sequential LLM Reasoning _run_ai_pipeline()

A sequential 4-step LLM pipeline threads context forward from one step to the next. Not one giant prompt — each step is a focused specialist call with minimal input/output. False positives dismissed by Step 3 are struck from the final display. Gracefully degrades — each step is optional; pipeline continues if any step fails.

Step 1: Protocol Classification Step 2: Flow Construction Step 3: Anomaly Validation + FP removal Step 4: Root Cause + Next Actions Risk Score 0–100 Priority Action
Step 1 · Protocol Classification
Input: protocol distribution, top flows, unusual port/protocol combos. Output: JSON list of classified behaviours, suspicious_protos, port mismatches.
Step 2 · Flow Construction
Input: per-source stats, TCP counters, ARP pairs, no-response stats, step-1. Output: JSON session list (completed/broken/one-way/attack), session health summary.
Step 3 · Anomaly Validation
Input: rule+ML findings, steps 1-2. Output: validated findings with AI confirmation, false_positive_titles list, new AI-only findings missed by rules.
Step 4 · Root Cause
Input: all prior steps. Output: incidents[] with root_cause, affected_hosts, next_actions (EXOS CLI), narrative paragraph, risk_score, priority_action.
🖥
08
Output Layer Interactive Browser Dashboard make_html()

Single-function HTML/CSS/JS generator. Every chart, terminal, packet table, and animation is written inline — no CDN calls, works fully air-gapped. 7 tab views + AI Analyst Sidebar with 5 specialist modes. WebSocket-based PTY terminal for live switch CLI access directly from the browser.

Overview · Packets · Protocols Anomalies · Traps · Timeline · Terminal Canvas2D charts (no Chart.js) WebSocket PTY terminal Export: JSON · CSV · PCAP Keyboard shortcuts (⌘K palette)
Packet Table
1000-row scrollable table. Filter by protocol, IP, port, MAC, VLAN, TCP flags. Sortable. Hex dump + layer tree detail pane with resize handle.
Flow Sequence Diagram
Canvas2D ladder diagram. REQ/RSP/DAT role badges. Protocol colour-coded arrows. Zoom + pan. Drag-resize. Click arrow to jump to packet.
Timeline
Interactive scatter plot (time vs bytes/pkt). Drag-to-pan, scroll-to-zoom, click-to-inspect. Protocol filter buttons. Hover tooltip.
Terminal
WebSocket PTY (bash). Auto-SSH to switch on load. connect/tconnect helpers. History. Ctrl-C/D/X/] buttons. Escape strip for clean output.
AI Analyst Sidebar

5 specialist AI modes

💡
Insights
auto on load

AI-generated capture summary with traffic behaviour tags, anomaly badge, risk score bar, protocol chips, and suggested next actions — all pre-computed before the user asks anything.

⚡
Explain
POST /api/chat · RAG

Click any detected protocol → AI generates a deep RFC-grounded explanation of that protocol in the context of this specific capture, backed by the RAG knowledge base.

📖
RFC
mode=rfc · RAG

RFC standards analysis for any protocol in the capture. Inline links to rfc-editor.org. RAG retrieves the relevant RFC chunks before the LLM answers. Manual RFC# lookup supported.

🔧
EXOS CLI
mode=exos · RAG

Select a protocol → AI generates a 7-section Switch Engine debug guide with exact CLI commands from the official Extreme Networks v33.6.1 PDF documentation. Never hallucinated.

💬
Chat
multi-turn · history

Free-form multi-turn conversation with full capture context injected. 20-turn memory. Switch management queries auto-routed to MCP live switch. AI backend switchable at runtime.

RAG Implementation

How the Knowledge Base enriches every AI answer

📡
PCAP Analysis
Detected protocols
→
🔍
RAG Retriever
Direct + semantic lookup
→
📚
Knowledge Base
RFC + EXOS chunks
→
✍️
Context Injection
Prepended to prompt
→
🤖
LLM Response
Grounded answer
🔐EAPoL / 802.1X + EXOS NetLogin
🔑RADIUS RFC 2865 + EXOS config
🌐ARP RFC 826 + DAI commands
🌀STP/RSTP 802.1D + EXOS stpd
📡LLDP 802.1AB + LLDP-MED
🚦OSPF RFC 2328 + EXOS ospf
🗺BGP RFC 4271 + EXOS bgp
📦VLAN 802.1Q tagged/untagged
🔗LACP/MLAG 802.3ad + sharing
📊SNMP RFC 3411 + trap config
🏠DHCP RFC 2131 + snooping
📋Your runbooks (auto-ingested)
▸ How ask_ai() routes and enriches every query
User sends chat message
         │
         ▼
ask_ai(prompt, protocols=detected_protos)
         │
         ├─ _is_mcp_query(prompt)?  ──yes──► _ask_mcp()  →  live EXOS switch data
         │
         ├─ protocols provided?
         │        │
         │        ▼
         │  _RAGRetriever.search(protocols)
         │  → Pass 1: direct protocol lookup  (score = 1.0)
         │  → Pass 2: TF-IDF / dense semantic search
         │  → Collect up to 8 chunks, max 6000 chars
         │  → Prepend as "## RFC Reference Material" block
         │  → Unknown protocols get ⚠️ disclaimer
         │
         ▼
enriched_prompt  =  RFC context  +  "## User Query"  +  original_prompt
         │
         ├─ AI_BACKEND == 'claude'  ──► _ask_claude(enriched_prompt)
         ├─ AI_BACKEND == 'openai'  ──► _ask_openai(enriched_prompt)
         └─ default                 ──► _ask_ollama(enriched_prompt)
Component Map

What's inside the two files

AI_PCAP_new_Apr27.py — Core
📦
read_pcap() + _parse_one()
Binary PCAP reader, L2–L7 decoder, EXOS header stripper
🔬
_extract_features()
L2–L7 statistical feature extraction into structured dict
🚨
_rule_engine() + _ml_score()
Deterministic rules + statistical anomaly scoring
🔗
_cross_layer_correlate()
Composite multi-signal pattern detection
🤖
_run_ai_pipeline() (4 steps)
Sequential LLM protocol classify → flow → validate → RCA
📚
_RAGRetriever (embedded)
TF-IDF index, KB loader, retrieve_rfc_for_protocols()
🔌
_ask_mcp() + MCP protocol
Stdlib SSE client → exos-mcp-server → live switch CLI
🖥
make_html()
Single-function dashboard generator, all assets inline
🌐
ThreadingHTTPServer
Serves dashboard + REST API + WebSocket PTY terminal
snmp_trap_analyzer.py
⚡
TrapRecord dataclass
Full trap data model: version, community, OID, varbinds, vendor, severity
📥
parse_trap_packets()
Ingests SNMP traps from main PCAP pipeline
📄
parse_trap_csv()
Standalone CSV/JSON trap export ingestion
📊
analyse_traps()
Link flap, auth storm, config storm, risk score 0–100
🎨
generate_traps_html()
6-section Traps tab: cards, charts, events, agents, security, AI button
knowledge_base/ (RAG corpus)
📋
rfc_index.json
Master KB index — extend with your own protocol entries
📁
exos/
EXOS CLI references, show command guides, release notes
📁
rfcs/
RFC protocol summaries chunked for retrieval
📁
runbooks/
Your internal troubleshooting runbooks — auto-ingested on startup
🔑
_embed_cache.json
Dense embedding cache — avoids re-encoding on restart
Workflow Detail

Key data flows in depth

📡 Live Switch Capture SSH / Telnet
A
Pre-flight Auth Check
SSH preferred, Telnet auto-fallback. Protocol=auto detects which is available.
B
Snapshot /usr/local/tmp via SFTP
Lists existing .pcap files before capture starts — used to detect the new file later.
C
Send debug packet capture on
Targets port / VLAN / interface. Capture runs for configured duration in background thread.
D
Poll SFTP until new .pcap appears
Retries up to 11× / 1s intervals. Verifies file size is stable before downloading.
E
SFTP Download (3-tier fallback)
sftp.get() → sftp.open()/read() → SCP. 90s hard timeout. 64KB chunks with progress.
F
Parse → Analyse → Reload
Full pipeline runs. Dashboard auto-reloads. Status polled via GET /api/capture-status.
🔌 MCP Live Switch Query stdlib SSE · JSON-RPC
1
Keyword Detection
_is_mcp_query() checks for vlan, ospf, bgp, show, firmware etc. → routes to _ask_mcp().
2
Raw SSE Socket Open
Stdlib socket — no mcp SDK. GET /sse → background reader thread parses event: endpoint.
3
Initialize + tools/list
JSON-RPC over POST to session URL. SSE queue delivers response. ~800 tools available.
4
Tool Selection
_pick_tool_for_query() matches query patterns → tool name. IP extracted from prompt if present.
5
tools/call → Live Switch
MCP server executes CLI on EXOS switch. Returns raw JSON/text output via SSE stream.
6
Ollama Formats Output
Raw switch output sent to local LLM for human-readable formatting. "[Live via MCP]" badge added.
Real-World Examples

NetScope in action

🏠 DHCP Server Failure High Severity
Input
dhcp_failure.pcap — client sends 3 DISCOVERs, receives no OFFER
Feature Extraction
dhcp_discover_count : 3 dhcp_offer_count : 0 dhcp_ack_count : 0
Rule Engine fires
{severity:"high", layer:"L7", category:"DHCP", title:"3 DISCOVERs, 0 OFFERs — server absent"}
AI Step 4 — EXOS Next Actions
1. show dhcpv4 server 2. show bootprelay 3. configure bootprelay add <server> vlan <n> 4. debug dhcp detail
⚡ SNMP Trap Storm Risk Score: 78
Input
trap_export.csv — 847 traps from 3 agents, 5 minutes
Trap Correlation
link_flaps : 12 (10.1.1.1 · port 2:1 · every 8s) auth_storms : 2 (10.1.1.5 · 18 authFailure in 45s) cfg_storms : 1 (10.1.1.1 · 16 saves in 60s via SNMP) weak_comm : ["public"]
Risk Score Breakdown
security ×15 = 30 | flaps ×10 = 20 auth ×15 = 28 | weak_comm = +10 → 78/100
AI Narrative
Config-change storm from 10.1.1.1 via SNMP suggests automated script. Auth-failure storm on 10.1.1.5 indicates brute-force. Rotate community strings, enable SNMPv3.
🔐 EAPoL Timing Race RAG Context
Detected protocols
EAPoL, RADIUS — ask_ai() triggers RAG lookup
RAG retrieves (chunk: EAPoL_timing_race)
After 'configure netlogin radius reauthentication resume', switch immediately sends EAP-Request/ Identity. Supplicant must be ready within 500ms or enters quiet-period (30s delay).
AI answer — grounded, not hallucinated
References the exact EXOS reauthentication resume command and 500ms window from the verified runbook chunk. Suggests adding client delay or passive receive loop.
EXOS mode verification command
show netlogin dot1x session debug netlogin
🛡 ARP + IP Spoofing (MITM) Critical · Cross-Layer
Rule Engine (L2)
ARP anomaly: 10.0.0.1 → 3 different MACs
Rule Engine (L3)
IP spoofing suspect: 10.0.0.1 seen from MACs: aa:bb:cc and dd:ee:ff and 11:22:33
Cross-Layer Correlation fires
{title: "ARP+IP Mismatch: MITM indicator", severity: "critical", layer: "L2+L3", detail: "ARP anomalies correlate with IP-to-MAC inconsistency — strong MITM indicator"}
AI EXOS remediation
configure iparp-inspection vlan <n> show iparp | show fdb mac <mac>
Technology

Tech stack

Layer Component Technology Notes
NETWORKSwitch ConnectivityParamiko · _Telnet (stdlib)SSH preferred, Telnet auto-fallback. No external Telnet library needed.
PARSEPCAP Binary ReaderPure Python structNo Scapy. Handles libpcap both endian, PCAP/PCAPNG. EXOS header auto-strip.
PARSEtshark Enrichmenttshark -T json (optional)Wireshark CLI. Degrades gracefully if absent. Adds DHCP names, SNMP OIDs, tunnels.
DETECTRule EnginePure Python dict30+ rules. Zero dependencies. Deterministic, auditable, no false negatives for known patterns.
DETECTML ScoringPure Python mathIsolation-Forest-inspired. Z-score deviation. No sklearn. Confidence score per finding.
AIRAG IndexTF-IDF (stdlib) or sentence-transformersAuto-upgrades to dense embeddings if library available. Cosine similarity search.
AILLM BackendsOllama · Claude API · OpenAI APIRuntime-switchable via UI. No restart needed. urllib.request only — no httpx/requests.
AIMCP Clientstdlib socket · threading · queueFull SSE + JSON-RPC wire protocol. No mcp SDK. Works with any Python 3.x.
SERVERHTTP ServerThreadingHTTPServerBuilt-in. No Flask/FastAPI. Handles concurrent WebSocket + REST requests.
SERVERPTY Terminalpty + WebSocket (Unix/macOS)Real bash PTY. Auto-SSH to switch. connect / tconnect helpers. History. ANSI stripping.
UICharts + CanvasVanilla JS Canvas2DDonut chart, scatter timeline, flow sequence diagram — all inline. Zero CDN.
UIDashboard HTMLHTML/CSS/JS (inline, make_html())Single file. IBM Plex Mono. Command palette. Keyboard shortcuts. Light/dark theme.
API Reference

REST + WebSocket endpoints

MethodPathDescription
GET/Serve dashboard. Shows welcome/setup page if no PCAP loaded yet.
POST/api/captureStart background switch capture. Body: {switch_ip, user, password, sw_port, vlan, duration, protocol}
GET/api/capture-statusPoll capture progress. Returns {running, phase, error, pcap}. Used by dashboard progress bar.
POST/api/capture-cancelForcefully stop a running capture and close SSH session.
POST/api/chatAI query — all sidebar modes. Passes detected protocols for RAG enrichment. Returns {response, via_mcp}.
POST/api/configUpdate AI backend/model/key at runtime without restart. Body: {backend, model, key}
POST/uploadUpload PCAP file (multipart/form-data). Validates magic bytes, re-analyses, returns {ok, file, packets, summary, anomalies}.
POST/upload-trap-csvUpload SNMP trap CSV/JSON. Triggers snmp_trap_analyzer pipeline. Returns {ok, traps, agents}.
POST/api/switch-pcapSwitch between original/uploaded PCAP slots without re-upload. Body: {slot: "original"|"uploaded"}
GET/export/jsonExport full analysis as JSON — packets (without hex/layers), proto_counts, anomalies.
GET/export/csvExport packet list as CSV with 18 standard fields (id, ts, proto, IPs, ports, flags, etc).
GET/export/pcapDownload the raw PCAP file currently loaded (original or uploaded slot).
WS/ws/terminalInteractive PTY terminal WebSocket. Bash shell, auto-SSH to switch on load. connect / tconnect helpers.
// NetScope · AI Network Analyzer
Python 3.9+ stdlib-only core ExtremeXOS v33.6.1 2 files · zero CDN Hema 2025–2026
Built for network engineers who want answers, not another dashboard.
